qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cloud function query string parameters override POST body parameters enabling authorization bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding describes a parameter override vulnerability in cloud function parameter handling (Object.assign merge order), which is unrelated to the advisory's root cause: missing request complexity limits for REST/GraphQL queries leading to resource exhaustion.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: File download and metadata endpoints require no authentication, exposing uploaded files to unauthenticated users
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding describes a missing authentication check on file download endpoints, which is unrelated to the advisory's root cause: missing request complexity limits leading to denial-of-service through resource exhaustion.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the qualifying findings identify the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-cmj3-wx7h-ffvg) describes a denial-of-service vulnerability caused by missing request complexity limits in Parse Server's REST and GraphQL APIs, leading to unbounded query nesting and resource exhaustion. The two qualifying findings describe completely different vulnerabilities: (1) parameter override in cloud functions and (2) missing authentication on file download endpoints. Neither finding addresses the missing requestComplexity limits, unbounded subquery depth, include depth, or GraphQL field selection limits described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-30946/GHSA-cmj3-wx7h-ffvg describes missing complexity limits: subqueryDepth, includeDepth, includeCount, graphQLDepth, graphQLFields
Advisory CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
Advisory impact: CPU, memory, database connection exhaustion through crafted complex queries
Finding 1: Parameter override via Object.assign({}, req.body, req.query) - unrelated to query complexity
Finding 2: Missing authentication on GET /files/:appId/:filename - unrelated to query complexity
